assertion_results.text: Each task file contains all required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements
assertion_results.evidence: All 7 task files checked. Each contains: Repository (e.g., 'trustify-backend'), Target Branch ('main'), Description (multi-sentence), at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes (with specific guidance), Acceptance Criteria (checkbox lists), and Test Requirements (checkbox lists). Task 1 has Files to Create + Files to Modify. Task 2 has Files to Modify only. Task 3 has Files to Create + Files to Modify. Task 4 has Files to Modify only. Task 5 has Files to Modify only. Task 6 has Files to Create + Files to Modify. Task 7 has Files to Modify only.

assertion_results.text: Task count is between 3 and 10 inclusive
assertion_results.evidence: There are exactly 7 task files: task-1-advisory-severity-summary-model.md, task-2-severity-aggregation-service.md, task-3-advisory-summary-endpoint.md, task-4-cache-invalidation.md, task-5-threshold-query-param.md, task-6-integration-tests.md, task-7-api-documentation.md. 7 is within [3, 10].
All 7 task files have '## Repository' followed by 'trustify-backend': task-1 line 2, task-2 line 2, task-3 line 2, task-4 line 2, task-5 line 2, task-6 line 2, task-7 line 2.

assertion_results.text: Task dependencies form a DAG with no circular dependencies
assertion_results.evidence: Dependency graph: Task 1 has no dependencies. Task 2 depends on Task 1. Task 3 depends on Task 2. Task 4 depends on Task 3. Task 5 depends on Task 3. Task 6 depends on Task 5. Task 7 depends on Task 3 and Task 5. This forms a DAG: 1->2->3->4, 3->5->6, 3->7, 5->7. No cycles exist.

assertion_results.text: Every task's Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths plausible within the repo-backend.md structure
assertion_results.evidence: Verified all paths against repo-backend.md directory tree. Files to Modify that exist in the tree: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs (task 1), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs (tasks 2, 5), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs (task 3), modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs (task 4), tests/Cargo.toml (task 6), README.md (task 7). Files to Create are in plausible locations: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/advisory_summary.rs (sibling to summary.rs, details.rs), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/advisory_summary.rs (sibling to list.rs, get.rs), tests/api/advisory_summary.rs (sibling to sbom.rs, advisory.rs). Task 5 modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/advisory_summary.rs which is created in Task 3 (a dependency).

assertion_results.text: Impact map lists concrete, specific changes (not vague or abstract descriptions)
assertion_results.evidence: The impact map in impact-map.md lists 9 specific changes under trustify-backend, each describing a concrete action: 'Add AdvisorySeveritySummary model struct for severity count response', 'Add service method to SbomService to aggregate advisory severity counts by SBOM ID with deduplication', 'Add GET /api/v2/sbom/{id}/advisory-summary endpoint handler', 'Register the new route in SBOM endpoint module', 'Add 5-minute cache configuration for the advisory-summary endpoint', 'Add cache invalidation in advisory ingestion pipeline when new advisories are linked to an SBOM', 'Add optional ?threshold query parameter to filter severity counts above a threshold', 'Add integration tests for the advisory-summary endpoint', 'Update API documentation to include the new endpoint'. All are specific and actionable.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention -- inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: Searched all 7 task files for convention applicability rationales in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'). No task contains any 'Applies:' rationale line. The Implementation Notes reference docs/constraints.md sections (e.g., 'Per docs/constraints.md section 5 (Code Change Rules)') but never include convention applicability rationales from CONVENTIONS.md per the convention-applicability-rules.md format. The repo-backend.md fixture lists a CONVENTIONS.md file in the repository tree, but no task includes convention enrichment with the prescribed rationale format.
